description: At Afterlife, Raina laments her transformation to Gordon and Lincoln, expressing feelings of monstrousness and suffering from nightmares involving Skye and her father. Gordon attempts to reassure her, describing how their gifts are layered and can lead to growth. The scene explores the psychological impact of transformation and feeling alienated.

transcript.line: Unless you're removing them, you're not helping at all.
Once your gifts have revealed themselves, we will train you how to use them.
I don't want to train. I want to leave. My only gift is to be the scary monster under the bed.
I can't even look in the mirror. My days are a pain. Every night, I'm assaulted by constant nightmares.
Stay out of it, spark plug. Don't get so wrapped up in your physical changes that you miss out on the gifts layered below.
What do you know about changes?
I've been through this. It's hard, but I promise our care is the best thing for you right now.
I'm tired of being caged. I want to feel fresh air.
He means well. You know, I might have something to help you sleep.

Sleep is easy. It's the relentless nightmares. I'm always being hunted like an animal, tangled in thorns. Or I dream of Skye. Skye and her father at dinner, champagne, a bouquet of daisies. And she's so... happy. She got everything and I got thorns.
I know this seems impossible, but, don't give up on us yet.
